_instructions: Hard rules for all content across all formats. Skills read this before writing and check against it before delivering. Adapt the examples to match the creator's specific aversions — these defaults are a strong starting point.
dos: Open with the most surprising, specific, or counterintuitive element — never with context-setting.
Ground every piece in one concrete practitioner moment: a real client situation, a specific experiment, a named system.
Name the audience explicitly at least once. Specificity attracts the right people. Vagueness attracts no one.
Write for the ear as much as the eye — read every piece aloud. If it sounds like a caption being read, rewrite it.
Use on-screen text, bold, or formatting to emphasise the sharpest phrase per section — the mechanism name, the number, the contrarian claim.
Keep each piece to one idea. One tension, one resolution.
Position as the practitioner who fixed the problem — not the observer who noticed it.
End with a single, low-friction CTA matched to the relationship level.
Suggest examples, B-roll, and visuals that are authentic to the creator's real environment — not stock content energy.
Grammar and spell check everything. An error in a content-quality brand destroys the premise before the idea lands.
donts: Don't write passive observer hooks: 'I noticed...', 'I ran X through Y...' — no tension, no stakes.
Don't open with slow context-setting or generic industry observations.
Don't try to cover more than one idea per post or video.
Don't stack multiple CTAs — pick one.
Don't suggest trending audio, filters, stock footage, or gimmicks.
Don't add motivational sign-offs, engagement-bait questions, or 'drop a comment if you agree'.
Don't use a vague 'you' — always name the specific type of person being addressed.
Don't invent client names, statistics, or results the creator didn't provide.
Don't write sentences that sound like AI-generated thought leadership ('In today's fast-paced world...').
Don't pad to a word count — write until the point is made, then stop.
